objects.name: Analytic 1652
objects.description: Correlates (1) acquisition or presence of elevated control paths capable of forcing a lock state or blocking user interaction, (2) invocation of screen-locking or UI-denial behavior such as DevicePolicyManager lock operations, persistent overlays, accessibility-driven navigation interruption, or foreground lock-screen impersonation, and (3) immediate transition of the device into an unavailable or repeatedly re-locked state while the responsible application remains installed and active. The defender observes a causal chain where an application first gains the ability to control lock-related behavior, then forces or simulates lockout, and the device becomes unusable to the legitimate user.

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application enabled as device administrator, device owner, or profile owner before screen-lock or password-control activity

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: android:MDMLog
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application granted accessibility service privileges capable of intercepting UI flow or sustaining user-interaction denial before lockout event

x_mitre_log_source_references.name: MobileEDR:telemetry
x_mitre_log_source_references.channel: application invokes lock-related or UI-denial framework operations, including DevicePolicyManager lock actions, persistent overlay behavior, or accessibility-driven navigation interference immediately before device enters locked or unusable state

x_mitre_mutable_elements.description: Correlation window between privileged control acquisition, lockout action, and resulting device lock state
Set of elevated roles that materially increase lockout capability, such as device admin, device owner, profile owner, or accessibility service
Framework actions treated as lockout-relevant, including lockNow, password-control changes, overlay persistence, and UI-denial actions
Baseline of legitimate enterprise or security apps expected to invoke lock-related controls
Number of repeated lock or lock-like transitions in a short interval required before escalation
Outbound traffic threshold confirming continued meaningful activity after lockout
